office_does: The sheriff leads county law enforcement, operates the jail, serves court process, and coordinates public-safety functions.
race_blurb: Two current local law-enforcement leaders differ most clearly in an enforcement-forward approach versus community policing and direct crisis assistance.

candidates.summary: Renzelman is Asotin's police chief and emphasizes collaborative community policing and immediate assistance for people in crisis.
highlights: Describes community policing as collaboration with residents and groups [S3]
Led a program providing food, fuel, and temporary lodging in crises [S4]

safety.basis: Community policing and officer-delivered crisis assistance place his documented approach toward prevention and services.

experience.basis: Current police-chief leadership establishes a substantial local command record.

candidates.summary: Richards is the county undersheriff and emphasizes warrants, drug enforcement, deputy staffing, visible policing, and accountability.
highlights: Prioritizes warrants, drug enforcement, staffing, and visible enforcement [S2]
Has county field-training and fire and EMS leadership experience [S3]

safety.basis: His explicit priorities center on warrants, drug enforcement, visible enforcement, staffing, and accountability.

experience.basis: Current undersheriff service and documented law-enforcement and emergency-service leadership establish a substantial record.
